title: Straightenable
summary: Defines invariants that admit evaluation through a chosen ambient model and proves closure operations at the interface level.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines invariants that admit evaluation through a chosen ambient model and proves closure operations at the interface level.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Invariants/Straightenable.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluation laws needed by the symbolic invariant calculus. Its mathematical data are: Associated: Tensor Invariant maps to Ambient Invariant maps to Prop; Is Natural: Tensor Invariant maps to Prop; Is Scalar Conformal: Scalar Invariant maps to Prop; Equal Modulo Divergence: Scalar Invariant maps to Scalar Invariant maps to Prop; XAnnihilates Last Index: Ambient Invariant maps to Prop; associated one: Associated scalar One.1 ambient Scalar One.1; associated tensor Prod: for every S T : Tensor Invariant St Tt : Ambient Invariant, Associated S St maps to Associated T Tt maps to Associated tensor Prod Invariant S T ambient Tensor Prod Invariant St Tt; associated contract: for every T : Tensor Invariant Tt : Ambient Invariant, Associated T Tt maps to for every C : Index Pairing, Associated contract Invariant T C ambient Contract Invariant Tt C.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{Associated}&\colon \operatorname{TensorInvariant} \longrightarrow \operatorname{AmbientInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Prop}\\\operatorname{IsNatural}&\colon \operatorname{TensorInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Prop}\\\operatorname{IsScalarConformal}&\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Prop}\\\operatorname{EqualModuloDivergence}&\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant} \longrightarrow \operatorname{ScalarInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Prop}\\\operatorname{XAnnihilatesLastIndex}&\colon \operatorname{AmbientInvariant} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Prop}\\\operatorname{associated\_one}&\colon \operatorname{Associated} 1_{\mathrm{scalarOne}} 1_{\mathrm{ambientScalarOne}}\\\operatorname{associated\_tensorProd}&\colon \forall {S T \colon \operatorname{TensorInvariant}} {\operatorname{St} \operatorname{Tt} \colon \operatorname{AmbientInvariant}}, \operatorname{Associated} S \operatorname{St} \longrightarrow \operatorname{Associated} T \operatorname{Tt} \longrightarrow \operatorname{Associated} (\operatorname{tensorProdInvariant} S T) (\operatorname{ambientTensorProdInvariant} \operatorname{St} \operatorname{Tt})\\\operatorname{associated\_contract}&\colon \forall {T \colon \operatorname{TensorInvariant}} {\operatorname{Tt} \colon \operatorname{AmbientInvariant}}, \operatorname{Associated} T \operatorname{Tt} \longrightarrow \forall C \colon \operatorname{IndexPairing}, \operatorname{Associated} (\operatorname{contractInvariant} T C) (\operatorname{ambientContractInvariant} \operatorname{Tt} C)\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Semantic naturality in the selected evaluation model. In explicit terms, for i of type tensor invariant, its defining expression is Invariant Evaluation Model.Is Natural I.
Scalar conformal covariance in the selected evaluation model. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is Invariant Evaluation Model.Is Scalar Conformal I.

A base invariant associated to a straight ambient invariant. Its mathematical data are: rank eq: I.rank equals Itilde.rank; weight eq: I.weight equals Itilde.weight; eval on einstein ambient: Invariant Evaluation Model.Associated I Itilde.

Scalar specialization of straightenable invariants. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, and itilde of type ambient scalar invariant, its defining expression is Straightenable I.1 Itilde.1.
Scalar Straightenable scalar One ambient Scalar One.
Tensor products preserve straightenable association. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_tensorProd [InvariantEvaluationModel] {S T : TensorInvariant} {St Tt : AmbientInvariant} (hS : Straightenable S St) (hT : Straightenable T Tt) : Straightenable (tensorProdInvariant S T) (ambientTensorProdInvariant St Tt)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Partial contractions preserve straightenable association. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_contract [InvariantEvaluationModel] {T : TensorInvariant} {Tt : AmbientInvariant} (hT : Straightenable T Tt) (C : IndexPairing) : Straightenable (contractInvariant T C) (ambientContractInvariant Tt C)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The Weyl tensor as a rank-four invariant of weight two. In explicit terms, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.riem rank : equals 4 weight : equals 2 parity : equals Invariant Parity.even.

The ambient Riemann tensor as a rank-four invariant of weight two. In explicit terms, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.riem rank : equals 4 weight : equals 2 parity : equals Invariant Parity.even.
The semantic input corresponding to Lemma 3.4. Its mathematical data are: associated: Invariant Evaluation Model.Associated Weyl Tensor Invariant Ambient Riemann Tensor Invariant.
Lemma 3.4: Weyl is associated to ambient Riemann curvature. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
Tensor power of the Weyl invariant. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | 0 maps to scalar One.1 | k plus 1 maps to tensor Prod Invariant weyl Power k Weyl Tensor Invariant.

Tensor power of ambient Riemann curvature. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | 0 maps to ambient Scalar One.1 | k plus 1 maps to ambient Tensor Prod Invariant ambient Riemann Power k Ambient Riemann Tensor Invariant.
For k in the natural numbers, weyl Power k.rank equals 4 times k.
For k in the natural numbers, weyl Power k.weight equals 2 times k : Int.
For k in the natural numbers, ambient Riemann Power k.rank equals 4 times k.
For k in the natural numbers, ambient Riemann Power k.weight equals 2 times k : Int.
For k in the natural numbers, Straightenable weyl Power k ambient Riemann Power k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem weylPower_straightenable [InvariantEvaluationModel] [WeylAmbientStraightness] (k : Nat) : Straightenable (weylPower k) (ambientRiemannPower k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A complete contraction of a rank-r tensor. Its mathematical data are: pairing: Index Pairing; complete: 2 times pairing.pair Count equals r.
A complete contraction of W to a scalar invariant.
correspondence.leanType: def WeylCompleteContraction (k : Nat) (C : CompleteContraction (4 * k)) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The corresponding complete contraction of ambient Riemann curvature.
correspondence.leanType: def AmbientRmCompleteContraction (k : Nat) (C : CompleteContraction (4 * k)) : AmbientScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For k in the natural numbers, and C of type Complete Contraction 4 times k, Weyl Complete Contraction k C.1.weight equals -2 times k : Int.
correspondence.leanType: theorem WeylCompleteContraction_weight (k : Nat) (C : CompleteContraction (4 * k)) : (WeylCompleteContraction k C).1.weight = -2 * (k : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Corollary 3.6: complete Weyl contractions are straightenable. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem WeylPolynomial_straightenable [InvariantEvaluationModel] [WeylAmbientStraightness] (k : Nat) (C : CompleteContraction (4 * k)) : ScalarStraightenable (WeylCompleteContraction k C) (AmbientRmCompleteContraction k C)

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
